claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my child. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to meet you. Let me ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you! 

**Are you responsible for Alex's upbringing?** This includes things like paying for their food, clothes, and day-to-day care.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of all of Alex's needs - food, clothes, school things, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for confirming that.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**What is your immigration or residency status?** Specifically, do you have the right to live in the UK (for example, are you a British citizen, have settled status, or have another form of leave to remain)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen living in the UK. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

One final question: **Is Alex in full-time education or training, or if not, are they under 16 years old?**

(Since you mentioned Alex is 8 years old, I expect this is straightforward, but I just want to confirm for the assessment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is only 8, so under 16. He's in primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information! Based on what you've told me, I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You are a British citizen living in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex is 8 years old and lives with you
- You are responsible for Alex's care (food, clothes, school needs, etc.)
- Alex is in full-time education (primary school)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **Age requirement**: Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
✅ **Residency**: You are a British citizen living in the UK
✅ **Responsibility**: Alex lives with you and you are responsible for his care and upbringing
✅ **No duplicate claims**: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. If you have any other questions about the process, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK, satisfying the residency requirement. Alex lives with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's upbringing (providing food, clothes, and day-to-day care). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with them. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and upbringing, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. All eligibility requirements under the Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

Alex lives with the claimant, and the claimant is responsible for Alex's upbringing (providing food, clothes, and day-to-day care). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with them. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and upbringing, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. All eligibility requirements under the Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
